DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Defendants LUAN LUU and ANDREW YEE's Demurrer to Complaint.

Appearances (via Zoom):

Carole Okolowicz, Esq., Coopers LLP, (415) 434-2111, for Plaintiff.

Jeffrey Garcia, Esq., Freeman Mathis & Gary, LLP, (213) 615-7000, for Defendants.

Ruling - ARGUED. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the subject motions. The court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is overruled. ***See Word minutes for complete ruling.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/CK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Defendants LUAN LUU and ANDREW YEE's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Appearances (via Zoom):

Carole Okolowicz, Esq., Coopers LLP, (415) 434-2111, for Plaintiff.

Jeffrey Garcia, Esq., Freeman Mathis & Gary, LLP, (213) 615-7000, for Defendants.

Ruling - ARGUED. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the subject motions. The court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' motion to strike punitive damages is denied. The court may strike (1) irrelevant, false, or improper matters inserted in any pleading, or (2) all or any part of any pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court. CCP sec. 436. Defendants move to strike Fitzsimmons's prayer for punitive damages. Punitive damages are permitted in tort actions on proof of malice, which is defined as "conduct which is intended by the defendant to cause injury to the plaintiff or despicable conduct which is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others." CCP sec. 3294(c)(1). The complaint alleges several incidents intentionally caused by Defendants which satisfy the prayer for punitive damages. First, the near-collision 'punishment pass' in which the driver allegedly passed Fitzsimmons "extremely closely in an intentional manner nearly knocking him from his bicycle." (Compl. 13.) Second, the collision, during which the driver accelerated into the back of Fitzsimmons's bicycle, ran over the bicycle, and speed away. (Compl. 14-17.) These plead defendants' despicable conduct with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of Fitzsimmons.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/CK)
